title: Wearable hydration tracking device with load-cell sensor
abstract: A portable container fitted with a load-cell sensor to measure liquid consumption over time, wirelessly transmitting data to a companion mobile application for hydration tracking and reminders.

title: Smart bottle cap with Bluetooth connectivity and consumption analytics
abstract: A bottle cap assembly comprising a pressure sensor and Bluetooth Low Energy module configured to estimate liquid volume consumed and sync data to a smartphone app for daily hydration goal tracking.

title: Method and system for gamified hydration reminders using mobile notifications
abstract: A software method for generating gamified reminders and rewards based on tracked liquid intake data received from a connected drinking vessel, encouraging users to meet daily hydration targets.

title: Portable container with integrated weight-based fluid level sensing
abstract: A drinking container integrating a strain-gauge based weight sensor at its base to continuously estimate remaining fluid volume, with an onboard display for real-time feedback.

title: AI-based crop irrigation scheduling using soil moisture sensors
abstract: A system for optimizing irrigation schedules for agricultural fields using machine learning models trained on soil moisture, weather, and crop-type data, deployed via edge sensors in the field.

title: Automated expense report classification using natural language processing
abstract: A method for automatically classifying and flagging anomalous expense report line items using NLP models trained on historical corporate expense data.

title: Multi-agent orchestration system for automated customer support workflows
abstract: A system comprising multiple cooperating software agents, each specialized for a subtask (intake, classification, resolution), coordinated by an orchestrator agent to resolve customer support tickets end to end.

title: Secure tool-calling framework for large language model agents
abstract: A framework for validating and rate-limiting tool invocations made by an autonomous language-model-driven agent, including sandboxing and permission scoping to prevent unauthorized actions.
